text.title: Defaults and constraints applied to all subagents
text.summary: A small, strict set of defaults applies to every subagent so no single phase can leak destructive or out-of-band side effects, and so commits pass the project's pre-commit hooks on the first try.

list.item: No remote `git push` without an explicit user instruction in the run.
No skipping git hooks (`--no-verify`, `--no-gpg-sign`). When a hook fails, fix the underlying issue and re-commit. NEVER amend; create a new commit.
No bulk `git add -A` / `git add .` — stage explicit paths. Bulk staging silently picks up local-only state (e.g. `scheduled_tasks.lock`, `.aidocs/catalog/improve-*.txt`) and secrets.
No commits to local-only files (preview data, dev toggles, environment overrides) unless the feature spec explicitly requires it.
No destructive operations against shared environments (DB writes, DAG triggers, schema changes, deploys, `git reset --hard`, force pushes) without explicit user authorization.
No `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer, no emojis in commit messages.
Move the feature folder through the SDD lifecycle: `todo/ → in-progress/ → done/`.
Move each `TASK_*.md` through the same lifecycle inside the feature folder.
Append a CHANGELOG entry under `## [Unreleased]` for every commit. The repo's pre-commit hook blocks commits without one.
Commit-title convention: 50 characters max, `<type>: <short description>` where type is `feat | fix | chore | refactor | docs | improve | test`. Optional body in present tense.
Run the appropriate auto-fix linter before staging: `ruff check . --fix && ruff format .` (Python), `npm run lint:fix && npm run format` (TS/JS).

section.title: Sensitive operations require confirmation
section.p: Operations that change shared state must round-trip through the user before execution. The orchestrator confirms via `AskUserQuestion`; the deployer subagent additionally verifies a confirmation token that the orchestrator threads through.

list.item: Running data-mutating jobs (DAG triggers, content reimports, batch updates) on `nero-prod` or `faion-net`.
Sending TG messages via `tg-send` to non-test channels.
Posting comments to GitHub issues / PRs.

rule.statement: If a target environment label is ambiguous (e.g. the user says "api" but `api.faion.net` and `api-dev.faion.net` both exist), the orchestrator MUST confirm the exact target with `AskUserQuestion` before any deploy or destructive op.
rule.rationale: Wrong-environment deploys are silent until they cause incidents. A two-second confirmation is always cheaper than a rollback. Same applies to ambiguous repos: monorepo `faionfaion/faion-net` (branch `master`) vs nested `faion-net-be` (branch `main`).

rule.statement: Between subagent dispatches, the orchestrator MUST read `/tmp/claude-session-state.json` and abort the wave when `rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage` ≥ user-set threshold (default 50%) or `rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage` ≥ 80%.
rule.rationale: Burning quota inside a single batch starves later batches in the same 5-hour window. Pause via `ScheduleWakeup` and resume at quota reset; do not amplify the cost of a long batch by ignoring its rate.
